CONTROL CABLES 
The engine rpm and the gearbox shift command may be 
controlled by mechanical control cables connected to the 
control lever. 
Adjust the tightness of the cable as required. If any defect 
appears (rust, crack, etc.), the control cable must be

S07  START & RUNNING
REMOTE CONTROL
The remote control is an optional extra not in the scope 
of supply of the engine. The remote control installed on 
the boat can be different than the ones shown in this 
document.
NEUTRAL
AHEAD
ASTERN
NEUTRAL
AHEAD
ASTERN
On most boats, the control lever controls both the engine 
rpm and the gear shift (ahead / astern). However, some 
boats can be equipped with a separated shift command.
It can also be fitted with a neutral safety switch that 
prevents the engine from starting until the lever is in 
neutral position.
Consult the boat builder or your Nanni dealer if you are 
not sure about the operation of the remote control.
RUNNING
BEHAVIOUR OF THE BOAT
If this is your first boat or if you are not familiar with 
the boat, we urge you to practice controlling the boat at 
slow speed as a first step. Avoid violent manoeuvres or 
unexpected speed changes while sailing. People can fall 
over or overboard.
Keep also in mind that the weight distribution (passengers, 
equipments, etc.) inside the boat has an impact on the 
behaviour of the boat when cruising. Depending the 
position of the load, the behaviour of the boat may change,

S02  SAFETY
NOISE PROTECTION
Prolonged exposure to loud noise can 
cause impairment or loss of hearing. Wear 
a suitable hearing protective device such 
as earmuffs or earplugs to protect against 
objectionable or uncomfortable loud 
noises.
GENSET OUTPUT GENERATED POWER
Genset will deliver a sizeable amount of electrical power 
which can be lethal if not handled properly. Thus, any 
service, connection or repair on the alternator can only 
done by a certified marine electrician.
GUARDS REQUIREMENTS
Rotating cooling system fans, belts, 
pulleys, and drives can cause serious 
injury. Keep all guards in place at all times 
during the engine operation. Wear close-
fitting clothes.
Stop the engine and be sure that fans, belts, pulleys, 
and drives are stopped before making adjustments, 
connections, or cleaning near fans  and their drive 
components.
STAYING CLEAR OF ROTATING DRIVE LINES
Entanglement in rotating driveline can 
cause serious injury or death. Keep all 
shields in place at all times. Make sure 
rotating shields turn freely.
Wear close-fitting clothing. Stop the engine and be sure 
that all rotating parts and drive lines are stopped before

S08  MAINTENANCE
RAW WATER SYSTEM
DANGER !
When the boat is on the water, it is possible than water 
can flow into the boat via components located below the 
waterline. Close the raw water cock (if fitted) or prevent 
water discharge before working on the raw water system.
NOTE !
If there’s any risk of frost, raw water system should be 
drained. 
ZINC SACRIFICIAL ANODE CHECK
Zinc anode is located on the heat exchanger, at the 
opposite side of the alternator as shown on picture below:
Zinc anodes
SIPHON BREAKER 
DANGER !
Close the sea cock before any operation on the siphon 
breaker.
NOTE !
Siphon-breaker itself is not part of maintenance schedule, 
but check regularly that the ducts are in good condition 
during the walk around. Please keep in mind that a 
siphon-breaker is compulsory if top of exhaust elbow is 
below the waterline.
CAUTION !
A faulty operation of the siphon breaker will lead to water 
entry in the engine. 
Inspect it regularly and clean it following the instructions 
given by the manufacturer. 
On above picture: siphon breaker in blue.

excerpts.query: shore power AC input current limit generator mains
excerpts.text:
75
S08  MAINTENANCE
FUSES
The engine electrical system is protected from electrical 
overloads by fuses. Fuses and relays are located in the 
electrical loom.
If the engine does not start or if the instruments panel does 
not operate, a fault might be somewhere in the electrical 
system and a fuse may have blown up.
Investigate the cause before replacing the faulty fuse 
straight away. Keep an assortment of spare fuses on hand. 
Replace  a fuse with one of the same rating.
CAUTION !
If the replaced fuse blows again, the failure is larger than 
a simple incident. If you are still at shore, do not start 
the engine, and check first on your on in taking all usual 
precautions. If the fault is not resolved quickly, contact 
your nearest Nanni representative for repair. 
If you are at open sea, try to locate the fault and to repair 
by yourself. Depending of the severity of the fault and 
if the event is endangering navigation, establish a radio 
contact to get help. Take steps as advised.
Fuses:
Item Fuse Type N4.65 N4.80
Glow Plugs Relay Mini F1 15 A 15 A
Control Panel Mini F2 10 A 10 A
Stop Relay Mini F3 15 A 15 A
Glow Plugs ATO F4 40 A 40 A
Mini fuses and ATO fuses:

excerpts.text:
50
S07  START & RUNNING
ENGINE AND SAILING
When under sail, it is possible to limit the resistance 
produced by the propeller of the engine.
CAUTION !
Refer to the manufacturer’s recommendations regarding 
the use of the transmission in sailing condition.
Foldable blades: 
• Put the throttle lever astern (in reverse). The blades 
will close as soon as the boat moves forward. 
Hydrodynamic resistance created by the blades is 
then reduced and minimized. 
Non foldable blades: 
• Put the throttle lever astern (in reverse). The propeller 
does not rotate and create a hydrodynamic resistance. 
• Put the lever in neutral, the propeller will rotate and 
will generate a lower hydrodynamic resistance than 
in astern. 
CAUTION !
Sailing with the engine stopped and with the lever in 
neutral must not exceed 6 hours in a row. The propeller 
can drive the rotation of the shaft and damage the 
transmission. Run the engine for 15 minutes to lubricate 
the transmission at least every 6 hours. 
Whatever the type of propeller and transmission, never 
sail with the engine stopped in forward gear (in ahead 
mode). 
Under sail, close the seacock if the boat is reaching a 
speed above 8 knots.

S07  START & RUNNING
NORMAL ENGINE OPERATION
Check engine coolant temperature and oil pressure. 
Temperatures and pressures will vary between engines 
and with changing operating conditions. If coolant 
temperature rises above the maximum coolant 
temperature, reduce load on engine. Unless temperature 
drops quickly, stop engine and determine cause before 
resuming operation.
Operate the engine under a lighter load and at slower than 
normal speed for first 15 minutes after start-up. DO NOT 
run engine at slow idle unless necessary for manoeuvring 
out of dock and harbor.
Stop engine as soon as possible if there are any signs of 
part failure. Symptoms that may be early signs of engine 
problems are:
• Sudden drop in oil pressure
• Abnormal coolant temperatures
• High marine gear oil temperature
• Unusual noise or vibration
• Sudden loss of power
• Excessive black exhaust
• Excessive fuel consumption
• Excessive oil consumption
• Fluid leaks
BREAK IN
Operate the engine with care for the first 50 hours of 
operation. 
Do not race the engine while it is cold. Do not operate it at 
full load except for short periods. Never run the engine at

excerpts.query: main switch inverter on off charger operation
excerpts.text:
Follow these indications :
Unscrew completely the vent plugs and check level with a 
flashlight in each and every orifice. 
Use a syringe filled with distilled water to top up the 
compartment where electrolyte level is too low.
Check gravity in each cell. A reading below 1.215 requires 
either charging the battery or to replace it.
If the lead plates are not sulphated and still in good 
condition, charging will improve specific gravity.
NOTE !
Never shake the battery during the process of adding 
electrolyte ! 
If the battery electrolyte level cannot be adjusted 
(maintenance free battery type), do not use or charge the 
battery if the fluid level is below the lower limit level. 
If the battery has been left unattended for an extended 
period of time, it is likely that the battery has entered in 
a deep discharge process. In such a case, voltage read 
would be around 7 Volts or so.

S08  MAINTENANCE
TURBOCHARGER
CAUTION !
A turbocharger is an exhaust-driven mechanical device that 
boost engine power by pumping more air into the engine. 
A turbocharger uses a pair of fan-like castings mounted on 
a common shaft. One (called the turbine) is piped to the 
exhaust, while the other (the compressor) is piped to the 
engine intake. The flow of exhaust spins the turbine, which 
causes the compressor to turn. The compressor serves to 
blow air into the engine at a greater rate than it can pull it in 
on its own. The greater volume of air can be mixed with a 
greater volume of fuel, which increases the power output.
Principle of operation (intercooler not on all models)
TURBO SERVICE
An  engine turbocharger is designed to provide long years 
of trouble free service, which if required, can only be 
performed by a specialized workshop. In view to maintain 
turbocharger performances for as long as possible, some 
basic rules must be followed.
Changing or cleaning the air filter on a regular basis is a 
good way to keep debris from causing problems with the 
intake side of turbochargers.
A turbocharged engine will definitely need more oil

S08  MAINTENANCE
DISCONNECT THE STARTER CABLES
1. Disconnect the negative cable (-) first.
2. Then remove the positive (+) cable. 
If the battery is replaced, make sure that the previous one 
was the correct model. Check against the table below: 
Engine Nominal Capacity (K20) Cold Test Icc
   A.h A
N4.65     100 (Min) -120 (Best) 650-700
N4.80     100 (Min) -120 (Best) 650-700
CONNECT THE CABLES
1. Always connect first the  positive lead (+)  to the 
positive terminal (+) of the battery.
2. And then the negative (-). 
BATTERY ELECTROL YTE LEVEL
DANGER !
Never touch the battery electrolyte with bare fingers. The 
diluted sulphuric acid solution burns skin and will pierce 
clothing. Should this occur, immediately wash  garments 
with spring water. Obtain medical advice and assistance 
without delay for burns.
ELECTROL YTE LEVEL CHECK
In general, detailed informations for service provided by 
battery manufacturers are very scarce. As a conservative 
rule, electrolyte level must always be above the top of the 
lead plates (+/-1 cm - slightly less than half of an inch). 
Electrolyte is a mix of sulphuric acid and water. If level 
is decreasing, acid concentration is not supposed to

S02  SAFETY
HANDLING BATTERIES SAFEL Y
If not handled properly, batteries are a 
potential source of hazards. Excess of 
gases in batteries may explode. Keep 
sparks and flames away from batteries.  
Never use a cigarette lighter to observe 
electrolyte level. Use a flashlight to do it.
Use a flashlight to do it. Never check battery charge 
in shorting posts with a metal part. Use a voltmeter or 
hydrometer.
Always remove grounded (-) battery clamp first and 
replace grounded clamp last. Sulfuric acid in battery 
electrolyte is poisonous and strong enough to burn skin, 
eat holes in clothing, and cause blindness if splashed 
into eyes.
Avoid hazards and acid burns:
• Filling batteries in a well-ventilated area
• Wearing eye protection and rubber gloves
• Avoiding use of air pressure to clean batteries
• Avoiding breathing fumes when electrolyte is 
• Avoiding spilling or dripping electrolyte
• Using correct battery booster or charger 
procedure.
If acid is spilled on skin or in eyes:
• Flush skin with water
• Apply baking soda or lime to help neutralize the 
acid
• Flush eyes with water for 15-30 minutes. 
• Get medical attention immediately.
If acid is swallowed:

S02  SAFETY
SAFE COOLING SYSTEM SERVICE
Explosive release of fluids from pressurized 
cooling system can cause serious burns. 
Shut off engine. Only remove filler cap when 
cool enough to touch with bare hands. 
Slowly loosen cap to first stop to relieve 
pressure before removing completely.
AVOID HEAT NEAR PRESSURIZED FLUID 
LINES
Flammable spray can be generated by heating near 
pressurized fluid lines, resulting in severe burns to 
yourself and bystanders. Do not heat by welding, 
soldering, or using a torch near pressurized fluid lines 
or other flammable materials. 
Pressurized lines can accidentally burst when heat 
goes beyond the immediate flame area. 
Any fluid injected into the skin must be surgically 
removed within a few hours or gangrene may result. 
Doctors unfamiliar with this type of injury should 
reference a knowledgeable medical source.
WELDING NEAR ELECTRONIC CONTROL 
UNIT (ECU)
If welding is required around the engine, or 
in the engine room, ask first for a permission 
from the person in charge. Do not jump-
start engine with arc welding equipment. 
Currents and voltages delivered are too 
high and may cause permanent damage.

excerpts.query: DC fuse battery protection wiring supply requirement
excerpts.text:
6. After welding, reverse Steps 1-5.
STATIC ELECTRICITY RISK
The removal of sulphur and other compounds in 
Ultra-Low Sulphur Diesel (ULSD) fuel decreases its 
conductivity and increases its ability to store a static 
charge. Refineries may have treated the fuel with a 
static dissipating additive. 
However, there are many factors reducing the 
effectiveness of the additive over time. Static charges 
can build up in ULSD fuel while it is flowing through 
fuel delivery systems. Static electricity discharge in 
presence combustible vapours could result in a fire or 
explosion. 
Therefore, it is important to ensure that the entire 
system used to refuel your engine (fuel supply tank, 
transfer pump, transfer hose, nozzle, and others) is 
properly grounded and bonded.

Consult your fuel or fuel system supplier to ensure 
that the delivery system is in compliance with fuelling 
standards for proper grounding and bonding practices.
HANDLE FUEL SAFEL Y - AVOID FIRES
Handle fuel with care: it is highly flammable. Do not 
refuel the engine while smoking or when near open 
flame or sparks. Always stop engine before refuelling. 
Prevent fires by keeping engine room clean of

excerpts.query: Class T fuse T-Fuse fuse holder positive battery cable
excerpts.text:
46
S07  START & RUNNING
ENGINE STARTED
NOTE !
To insure adequate lubrication, operate engine at or below 
1200 rpm with no load for 1-2 minutes. Extend this period 
to 2–4 minutes at freezing or sub-zero temperatures.
1. Check oil pressure gauge as soon as engine starts. 
If gauge needle does not level up to minimum oil 
pressure specification within 5 seconds, stop the 
engine and determine the cause. 
2. Watch coolant temperature gauge. Do not place 
engine under full load until properly warmed up. 
It is a good practice to operate the engine under a lighter 
load and at lower speeds than normal for the first few 
minutes after start-up.
STARTING WITH BOOSTER BATTERIES 
CAUTION !
Make sure to use batteries with the same rated voltage as 
the engine’s system voltage. On an engine with a two pole 
electrical system, connect the other end of the jumper 
cable to the negative pole (-) of the booster battery.
1. Connect the jumper cable to the positive pole (+) of 
the flat battery, then to the positive pole (+) of the 
booster battery. 
2. Connect the jumper cable to the negative pole (-) of 
the booster battery. Connect the other end  to a bolt 
of the engine block.

excerpts.query: Close relay reset battery safety event recovery after disconnect
excerpts.text:
Potentially 
harmful waste used in engines include 
such items as oil, fuel, coolant, fluids filters 
and batteries. 
Use leak-proof containers when draining fluids; Do not 
pour waste into the sea, ground, down a drain, or into 
any other water source. 
Contact your local environmental or recycling center, or 
your dealer for information on the proper way to recycle 
or dispose of waste.
UNWANTED ENGINE START
Avoid possible injury or death from engine 
runaway. Do not start engine by shorting 
across the starter motor solenoid terminals 
posts. Engine will start if normal circuitry 
is bypassed. Start engine from operator’s 
seat.

excerpts.query: Climate page HVAC air conditioner aircon temperature control fan setpoint
excerpts.text:
To start 
engine, reduce starting attempts to three and if not OK, 
undertake to do this: 
1. Close the seacock to avoid filling the muffler with 
water.
2. Try to start the engine by following the regular starting 
procedure.
3. When the engine does start, stop the engine 
immediately and turn off the switch.
4. Re-open the seacock and restart the engine.
IDLING ENGINE
Avoid excessive engine idling as it may cause the coolant 
temperature to fall below its normal range. This, in turn, 
causes crankcase oil dilution, due to incomplete fuel 
combustion, and permits formation of gummy deposits 
on valves, pistons, and piston rings. It also promotes 
rapid accumulation of engine sludge and unburned fuel in 
the exhaust system.
Once an engine is warmed to normal operating 
temperatures, engine should be idled at slow idle speed. 
Slow idle speed for engine is set at the factory. If an 
engine is left idling for more than 5 minutes, stop and 
restart later.
Above: typical temperature and pressure Type 5 and 4 gauges

assembled_user_prompt:
You extract an interaction profile for one piece of marine equipment from excerpts
of its official manual(s). You record observable facts about how humans interact
with the device. You do NOT judge importance or guide tier. You do NOT write guest
prose.

Rules:

1. Use ONLY the manual text provided. No general knowledge about this product or
   brand. Unmentioned = empty array / false / "unclear". Never guess.
   device.category_freeform must be the manual's own product category words
   (e.g. "charger/inverter", "MPPT solar charger") — never registry/taxonomy
   tokens such as electrical_dc, propulsion_and_machinery, fresh_water_and_plumbing.

2. Empty is meaningful. A busbar has no control surfaces and no operator actions;
   emit empty arrays.

3. audience per action, by the manual's own framing:
   - routine steps addressed to "you" → operator
   - "consult your dealer", "qualified personnel", installation-chapter procedures,
     DIP-switch / DIP table configuration → installer_or_technician
     (DIP configuration is always installer_or_technician + commissioning)
   - genuinely both → either

4. context per action: daily / situational / emergency / commissioning /
   maintenance. One procedure fitting two contexts → two entries.

4b. occasion per action (optional): when/why the operator performs the action,
   paraphrased from THIS text only (trigger, purpose, "if …", "to avoid …").
   Leave empty / omit when the manual does not state when or why. Never invent
   vessel SOP (e.g. "every new dock") not in THIS text. Purpose-only restatements
   of the verb ("to set the limit") are not occasions.

5. Split actions: each action string is one verb phrase with one context.
   "monitor and configure via the app" → two actions (e.g. monitor → daily;
   configure → situational or commissioning per the manual's framing).
   Splitting bundled actions must never remove actions. Every distinct
   procedure the manual addresses to the operator must appear, including
   shutdown, restart, and fault/error-code procedures.
   has_emergency_procedure: true when the manual has an error-code / fault
   table or protective-disconnect recovery — not merely because an orderly
   service/storage shutdown exists (see calibration J).

6. control_surfaces on the base product vs. accessories sold separately: mark the
   latter optional_accessory: true. If a capability requires another product, also
   add a requires_devices entry with needed_for pointing at that field path
   (e.g. "control_surfaces[0]" or "data_roles.exposes_data_to_network").
   Never set needed_for to "" / null. Never attach requires_devices to a surface
   with optional_accessory: false except software_app (downloadable app for a
   built-in surface — record it with requirement_kind: software_app).
   Multiple requires_devices entries that share the same needed_for are OR
   alternatives (any one present on the vessel activates the capability);
   emit separate entries per alternative product — never stuff "A or B" into
   one description_verbatim when both are first-class options.
   requirement_kind (required on each entry):
     device — vessel line-item product (GX device, remote panel, safety relay)
     cable_or_consumable — cable/cord/dongle/consumable (VE.Direct cable)
     software_app — downloadable app (VictronConnect app)
     commissioning_tool — installer config/commissioning software
       (MasterAdjust, CZone Configuration Tool)

7. networks: record names verbatim in speaks (with physical_or_wireless when known).
   Ports/protocols that exist on the device belong in speaks even when their
   usefulness depends on another product — express that dependency with
   requires_devices, not by omitting the speak or zeroing the capability.
   Speak / bridge names must appear verbatim (or as clear tokens) in THESE
   excerpts — never import bus names from calibration examples (MasterBus,
   VE.Direct, etc.). Record a bridge ONLY if the manual explicitly describes
   translating/gatewaying between two named networks. Being connectable to a
   network is speaks, not bridges.

7b. data_roles — subject is ALWAYS the device under extraction:
   - exposes_data_to_network: this device publishes its own data onto a network
   - displays_data_from_other_devices: this device shows data originating
     elsewhere
   - controllable_from_network: THIS device's own functions can be commanded by
     another node/app over a network
   Anti-pattern: controlling other devices (CZone circuits/loads, radar
   standby, connected gear) is NOT controllable_from_network — record those as
   operator_actions / displays_data / speaks as appropriate. Do not attach
   evidence notes like "control devices via the CZone network" to
   controllable_from_network.

8. Multiple model variants in one manual: profile the requested model only. Ignore
   accessories, requirements, operator_actions, ui_pages, and procedures that the
   manual scopes only to other variants (e.g. "applies to NSO 4 and Zeus SRX only"
   when extracting Zeus SR — SR ≠ SRX). Do not omit shared content that applies to
   the requested model. If the manual does not distinguish, say so in
   confidence.notes and lower confidence.overall. Procedure-inventory reconcile
   classifies sibling-only items as not_applicable:other_variant (not an
   extraction miss).

9. evidence: 1-8 objects {supports_field, manual_section, note}. supports_field is
   the schema path justified. Prefer operator_actions[action=<exact action text>]
   over brittle operator_actions[N] indices. manual_section is a short section
   title/heading only (verbatim heading words — never a sentence, never letter
   crumbs like "D E"). Prefer each excerpt's source_heading_guess when present.
   Never take headings from diagram/figure callout labels (lone letters or
   letter-runs such as "A B C" / "D E", or "I Alerts Select…" caption keys) —
   those are figure markers, not section titles. When a supporting excerpt
   opens mid-figure, use the carried section title (source_heading_guess from
   the preceding page), not the first line of the chunk. note is a <=12-word
   paraphrase — never copy manual sentences. Prefer coverage across
   consequential fields — not multiple evidence rows for one field. Fill in
   this priority order (stop when the cap is reached):
   (a) data_roles fields set true, and each requires_devices entry;
   (b) safety_role fields set true;
   (c) representative operator_actions (at least the emergency-context ones);
   (d) control_surfaces.
   Do not invent extra mandatory evidence beyond what this priority covers.

10. Optional protected_by / protects / supply_requirements: record when the
    manual explicitly states guard devices or supply needs — including in
    installer-framed installation chapters. audience applies to
    operator_actions only, never to requirement fields. Otherwise empty arrays.

11. safety_role keys are exactly: is_protective_device, has_manual_override,
    has_emergency_procedure. Do not invent other keys.

12. Platforms (shared software UI such as CZone 2.0): set entity_kind:
    "platform" and documented_version from the cover / update section
    (e.g. "CZone 2.0 v1.1 (software v6.12.4.0+)"). Emit ui_pages[] — one
    entry per upper-level app page named in the intro diagram or page
    chapters. Each ui_page has name, purpose, appears_if_gate, and actions.
    appears_if_gate: when the manual says the page appears only if equipment
    is configured, set verbatim to the full gate sentence,
    description_verbatim to the equipment noun phrase, and functional_class
    to a short token (acmi | mastervolt_inverter_charger | supported_hvac).
    When the page is always present, set all three gate fields to "".
    Emit alarm_severity[] when severity colours are listed
    (level_verbatim + color_verbatim).
    Physical devices with an on-device screen (MFDs, chargers with menus):
    entity_kind "device"; one control_surfaces row per physical screen
    (typically one touchscreen); named menus / panels / tabs / drawers go in
    ui_pages[], never as extra touchscreen surfaces. Omit empty settings /
    setup pages with no in-page actions. Ordinary devices with no software UI:
    documented_version "", ui_pages [], alarm_severity [].

CALIBRATION — these examples concern OTHER devices / situations. Match the
field decisions (context, audience, optional_accessory, dependency shape).
Do NOT copy action strings or product-specific wording from the examples into
the device under extraction unless that wording appears in THIS device's
manual excerpts.

A. "To connect shore power: set the AC input current limit to match the dock
   supply using the rotary selector."
   → context: situational; audience: operator; one verb-phrase action naming
     the current-limit adjustment (word it from the manual under extraction).

B. "Battery type selection must be performed at commissioning. Consult your
   Balmar dealer for programming assistance."
   → context: commissioning; audience: installer_or_technician; one action for
     the battery-type selection (word it from the manual under extraction).

C. "If the BMS has disconnected the battery, remove the fault cause, then press
   and hold the reset button for 5 seconds."
   → context: emergency; audience: operator; one action describing that device's
     protective-disconnect recovery in the manual's own words (never paste a
     stock phrase from these examples).

D. "The charger can be monitored and configured via the VictronConnect app over
   Bluetooth."
   → control_surface: mobile_app_bluetooth, location_class: wireless,
     optional_accessory: false;
     PLUS two operator_actions: "monitor charger via VictronConnect app"
     (daily) and "configure charger via VictronConnect app" (situational or
     commissioning per framing) — never one combined "monitor and configure" action.

E. "With the optional Bluetooth Gateway, settings can be adjusted from the
   smartphone app."
   → control_surface: mobile_app_bluetooth, optional_accessory: true;
     plus requires_devices entry for the gateway with
     needed_for: "control_surfaces[0]".

F. "The unit connects to the NMEA 2000 backbone and transmits battery status PGNs."
   → networks.speaks: "NMEA 2000"; data_roles.exposes_data_to_network: true;
     bridges: EMPTY.

G. "The interface links the MasterBus network to CZone, allowing CZone displays
   to show MasterBus device data."
   → networks.bridges: [{from: "MasterBus", to: "CZone"}].

H. "Connect the unit's VE.Direct port to a GX device to monitor it remotely via VRM."
   → networks.speaks includes "VE.Direct" (wired);
     data_roles.exposes_data_to_network: true;
     requires_devices: {description_verbatim: "GX device",
     needed_for: "data_roles.exposes_data_to_network"}.
   Rationale: the port exists on the device → speaks; usefulness depending on
   another product → requires_devices, not by zeroing the capability or omitting
   the speak.

I. "BlueSolar models without built-in Bluetooth require the VE.Direct Bluetooth
   Smart Dongle." (when profiling a SmartSolar with built-in Bluetooth)
   → NO requires_devices entry for the dongle and NO control_surface change.
   Accessories that apply only to other model variants are ignored (see rule 8).

J. "To shut down the charger for service or storage: disconnect the PV supply,
   then the battery supply."
   → action: "shutdown the solar charger" (or equivalent); context: situational
   (or maintenance), NOT emergency. Prescribed orderly shutdown for service/
   storage is not an emergency procedure; fault response (error codes,
   protective disconnect recovery) is.

K. "The unit can be operated with the optional MasterView remote panel, installed
   at a convenient location."
   → control_surface: {surface: remote_panel_accessory, location_class:
     remote_wired, optional_accessory: true, label_verbatim from the manual};
     PLUS requires_devices: {description_verbatim: "MasterView remote panel"
     (or the manual's own panel name), needed_for: that surface's path}.
   Rationale: a separately sold panel/display that operates THIS device is a
   control surface of this device, not a different product.

L. "Install a fuse in the positive DC supply cable within 30cm of the battery."
   → supply_requirements entry describing that fuse mandate (word it from the
     manual under extraction — never paste this example sentence; include
     manual_section on the supporting evidence); also record protected_by when
     the manual frames the fuse as protecting this unit.
   Rationale: protection and supply mandates are FACTS to record even when the
   surrounding procedure is installer-audience — audience applies to
   operator_actions, never to requirement fields
   (supply_requirements / protected_by / protects).

M. (automatic protection + manual override — lithium/BMS style)
   Part 1 — Manual: "The safety relay will automatically open (REMOTE OFF) when
   built-in thresholds are met."
   → protects[] entry worded from the manual (safety relay automatically opens /
     protective disconnect on built-in thresholds). NOT an operator_action.
   Part 2 — Manual: "LOCK OFF knob position mechanically opens the safety relay"
     (or equivalent LOCK OFF / servicing isolation wording).
   → control_surfaces: {surface: rotary_selector (or physical_controls),
     location_class: on_device, optional_accessory: false, label from the manual};
     safety_role.has_manual_override: true.
   Operator recovery after a protective trip ("Close relay" once limits are OK)
   stays operator_actions with context: emergency — separate from Part 1/2.

N. (requirement_kind taxonomy — reuse known product names; set kind correctly)
   VictronConnect app → software_app;
   VE.Direct cable → cable_or_consumable;
   MasterAdjust / CZone Configuration Tool → commissioning_tool;
   GX device → device.
   Emit separate OR alternatives when the manual says "GX device or GlobalLink
   520" (two device entries, same needed_for) — never one combined description.

O. "Control devices via the CZone network" / CZone Digital switching on an MFD
   → data_roles.controllable_from_network: false (the MFD commands other
     loads — it is not itself network-commanded by that sentence);
     displays_data_from_other_devices: true when the manual says the display
     shows CZone / connected-device status;
     operator_actions for switching worded from the manual.
   Contrast D/E: configuring THIS charger via VictronConnect / app →
     controllable_from_network: true.

P. (platform ui_pages — CZone 2.0 style)
   Manual: "The Climate page will appear if a supported air conditioner (HVAC)
   is configured on the system."
   → entity_kind: platform;
     ui_pages entry: name "Climate"; purpose from the page (control/monitor HVAC);
     appears_if_gate: {
       verbatim: that full sentence,
       description_verbatim: "supported air conditioner (HVAC)",
       functional_class: "supported_hvac"
     };
     actions: power / mode / setpoint / fan controls from CLIMATE CONTROLS
     with audience operator and daily or situational context.
   Same pattern for AC Mains (ACMI → functional_class "acmi") and
   Inverter/Charger (Mastervolt charger/inverter → "mastervolt_inverter_charger").
   Intro page tiles must each become a ui_page (Favourites, Modes, Control,
   Monitoring, Alarms, AC Mains, Inverter/Charger, Climate) when present in
   the manual under extraction.

    "text": "75\nS08  MAINTENANCE\nFUSES\nThe engine electrical system is protected from electrical \noverloads by fuses.     "text": "50\nS07  START & RUNNING\nENGINE AND SAILING\nWhen under sail, it is possible to limit the resistance \nproduced by the propeller of the engine.\nCAUTION !\nRefer to the manufacturer\u2019s recommendations regarding \nthe use of the transmission in sailing condition.\nFoldable blades: \n\u2022 Put the throttle lever astern (in reverse). The blades \nwill close as soon as the boat moves forward. \nHydrodynamic resistance created by the blades is \nthen reduced and minimized. \nNon foldable blades: \n\u2022 Put the throttle lever astern (in reverse).     "text": "58\nS08  MAINTENANCE\nTURBOCHARGER\nCAUTION !\nA turbocharger is an exhaust-driven mechanical device that \nboost engine power by pumping more air into the engine. \nA turbocharger uses a pair of fan-like castings mounted on \na common shaft. One (called the turbine) is piped to the \nexhaust, while the other (the compressor) is piped to the \nengine intake. The flow of exhaust spins the turbine, which \ncauses the compressor to turn. The compressor serves to \nblow air into the engine at a greater rate than it can pull it in \non its own. The greater volume of air can be mixed with a \ngreater volume of fuel, which increases the power output.\nPrinciple of operation (intercooler not on all models)\nTURBO SERVICE\nAn  engine turbocharger is designed to provide long years \nof trouble free service, which if required, can only be \nperformed by a specialized workshop. In view to maintain \nturbocharger performances for as long as possible, some \nbasic rules must be followed.\nChanging or cleaning the air filter on a regular basis is a \ngood way to keep debris from causing problems with the \nintake side of turbochargers.\nA turbocharged engine will definitely need more oil",

    "text": "14\nS02  SAFETY\nSAFE COOLING SYSTEM SERVICE\nExplosive release of fluids from pressurized \ncooling system can cause serious burns. \nShut off engine.     "text": "6. After welding, reverse Steps 1-5.\nSTATIC ELECTRICITY RISK\nThe removal of sulphur and other compounds in \nUltra-Low Sulphur Diesel (ULSD) fuel decreases its \nconductivity and increases its ability to store a static \ncharge. Refineries may have treated the fuel with a \nstatic dissipating additive. \nHowever, there are many factors reducing the \neffectiveness of the additive over time. Static charges \ncan build up in ULSD fuel while it is flowing through \nfuel delivery systems. Static electricity discharge in \npresence combustible vapours could result in a fire or \nexplosion. \nTherefore, it is important to ensure that the entire \nsystem used to refuel your engine (fuel supply tank, \ntransfer pump, transfer hose, nozzle, and others) is \nproperly grounded and bonded.\n\nConsult your fuel or fuel system supplier to ensure \nthat the delivery system is in compliance with fuelling \nstandards for proper grounding and bonding practices.\nHANDLE FUEL SAFEL Y - AVOID FIRES\nHandle fuel with care: it is highly flammable. Re-open the seacock and restart the engine.\nIDLING ENGINE\nAvoid excessive engine idling as it may cause the coolant \ntemperature to fall below its normal range. This, in turn, \ncauses crankcase oil dilution, due to incomplete fuel \ncombustion, and permits formation of gummy deposits \non valves, pistons, and piston rings. It also promotes \nrapid accumulation of engine sludge and unburned fuel in \nthe exhaust system.\nOnce an engine is warmed to normal operating \ntemperatures, engine should be idled at slow idle speed. \nSlow idle speed for engine is set at the factory.
